Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast for KL, Labuan and 9 states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds involving the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Labuan as well as nine other states until 9pm today.
It said in a statement that adverse weather is set to hit Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling); Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Mualim); and Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman).
Other peninsula states set to be affected are Selangor (Hulu Selangor and Gombak); Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan and Pekan); Negri Sembilan (Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, Jempol and Tampin); and Johor (Batu Pahat and Pontian).
In Sabah, the areas likely to be affected are the Interior (Sipitang, Tenom, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort, Keningau and Tambunan); the West Coast (Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu and Ranau); and Sandakan (Beluran).
In Sarawak, similar conditions are expected in several districts, including Sri Aman, Sibu (Sibu and Selangau); Mukah (Dalat and Mukah); Kapit; Bintulu; Miri and Limbang.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Everyone must respect election rules, ethics during Johor, Negri Sembilan polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded all parties to respect campaign rules and ethics during the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections.
He also requested that the Election Commission carry out its responsibilities effectively and independently to ensure the smooth running of the elections in both states.
"Now, when campaigning begins, I hope it will be well-managed and that the EC carries out its responsibilities effectively and independently.
"Earlier, after the cabinet meeting, I managed to speak with colleagues about upholding the rules and respecting campaign ethics," he said when met after Friday prayers at Al-Hidayah mosque, here.
Earlier, the EC set
July 11
as polling day for the Johor election, with nomination day set for June 27.
Negri Sembilan, meanwhile, will go to the polls on
Aug 1
, with the EC setting July 18 as nomination day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: MCMC forwards complaints to social platforms without bias
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) forwards complaints related to social media content to the relevant platforms without bias, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said.
He said each social media platform has its own community guidelines, and MCMC's role as a statutory body is to bring public complaints to their attention.
"For MCMC, it receives complaints and forwards them to the platforms without bias, without fear or favour, and without being influenced by any party.
"Ultimately, it is up to the platform to decide whether to remove the content or not. Not all content that MCMC brings to the attention of social media platforms is taken down; in fact, many are not removed," he said at his weekly press conference.
He was responding to allegations that several activists and online content creators critical of the government had their posts taken down or were being investigated by MCMC.
He added that the public should not rely solely on MCMC to report content.
"They can also directly file reports with the platforms themselves. On each platform, there is a button that allows anyone to report content."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muhyiddin's ex-aide rejects Pas accusation of greed in seat negotiations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former aide to Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, Datuk Dr Marzuki Mohamad, has questioned Pas' claim that Bersatu was greedy in the allocation of electoral seats.
Marzuki, who represented Bersatu in seat negotiations from the Pakatan Harapan 1.0 administration through to the Perikatan Nasional (PN) era, said seat distribution among component parties followed an established process based on specific formulas.
"The process begins at the state level, where representatives from the parties discuss the seats each will contest. Usually, about 50 per cent of negotiations are settled at this stage.
"The remaining 50 per cent, involving overlapping claims among component parties, is then referred to the central seat negotiation committee for resolution," he said in a Facebook post today.
Pas yesterday revealed several factors it claimed prompted its central leadership to sever political ties with Bersatu, including alleged attempts to weaken and undermine the party.
Its president, Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, was reported as saying that the loosest form of cooperation between Pas, Bersatu and other parties in the past was aimed solely at taking over the federal government through statutory declarations.
Marzuki said seat allocations were guided by the principle that incumbent seats remained with the party that previously held them.
He said allocations also took into account each party's influence and organisational strength.
"Pas contested more seats in Malay-majority states such as Kelantan, Terengganu and Kedah, where it had stronger grassroots support.
"In west coast states, Bersatu and Pas contested a nearly equal number of seats due to their comparable strength," he said.
Marzuki said seat allocations in PN's Grade B constituencies were based on each party's relative influence, with Pas holding an advantage in some areas and Bersatu in others.
He added that Bersatu helped balance Pas' Islamist-conservative image in Malaysia's multi-racial and multi-religious society.
"This was the formula used by the PN seat allocation committee, which also contributed to Pas' landslide victories in the 15th General Election and the 2023 state elections, as acknowledged by the Pas president himself."
He said about 95 per cent of seat allocation matters were resolved by the PN seat allocation committee, while the remaining five per cent of overlapping claims were referred to the Presidential Council.
"After the Presidential Council makes its decision, the seat allocation list is prepared and signed by all party presidents.
"Muhyiddin, as PN chairman, then signs the candidate appointment letters based on the agreed list.
"Although he holds the authority to issue these letters, he has never issued one to a Bersatu candidate if the seat had already been allocated to Pas by the Presidential Council," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Farm Fresh MD buys into dip as shares hit nine-month low
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Farm Fresh Bhd's co-founder, group managing director and chief executive officer Loi Tuan Ee has increased his stake in the dairy company through open-market purchases.
The share accumulation comes as Farm Fresh's stock trades near its lowest level in about nine months.
In its filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said Loi bought 100,000 shares on June 10 at RM2.18 per share and another 100,000 shares on June 11 at RM2.16 per share.
The purchases, valued at about RM434,000 in total, raised his direct shareholding in the company to 0.058 per cent.
Loi also holds an indirect interest of more than 37.3 per cent in Farm Fresh through investment vehicles Farmchoice Foods Sdn Bhd and Rainforest Capital Sdn Bhd.
Last month, Farm Fresh reported a 22 per cent increase in net profit to an all-time high of RM129.6 million for the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26).
This was supported by stronger domestic demand, new product launches and expanding distribution channels.
The company also surpassed RM1 billion in annual revenue for the first time since its listing in 2022.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold futures reverse losses to end higher
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Gold futures on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives pared yesterday's losses to end higher on Friday, in line with firmer US COMEX performance, amid a weakening US dollar.
SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the weaker greenback affected market sentiment and resulted in lower US Treasury yields.
"Bullion is also drawing support from its positive correlation with US equities," he told Bernama.
At the close, the spot-month June 2026 contract increased to US$4,232.70 per troy ounce from US$4,091.50 per troy ounce at Thursday's close; July 2026 rose to US$4,249.30 per troy ounce from US$4,106.50, and August 2026 increased to US$4,265.70 per troy ounce from US$4,124.20.
The September 2026 contract rose to US$4,270.20 per troy ounce from US$4,128.70 on Thursday, while the October 2026 contract grew to US$4,289.30 per troy ounce from US$4,147.80 per troy ounce previously.
Trading volume expanded to 22 lots from 18 lots on Thursday, while open interest inched up to 89 contracts from 88 contracts previously.
Meanwhile, physical gold was fixed at US$4,074.85 per troy ounce at the London Bullion Market Association (LBMA) afternoon fix on June 11, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MAG group CEO Captain Nasaruddin appointed to IATA board of governors
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar has been appointed to the board of governors (BoG) of the International Air Transport Association (IATA).
MAG and Malaysia Airlines Bhd non-executive director Datuk Amirul Feisal Wan Zahir said Nasaruddin's appointment is a testament to his deep expertise, leadership and long-standing contributions to the aviation industry.
"I am confident that he will bring valuable insight and perspective to the Board, further strengthening Malaysia's voice in global aviation and supporting the continued advancement of the industry," Amirul said in a statement.
According to MAG, said the appointment was made during IATA's 82nd annual general meeting (AGM) in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.
It noted that Nasaruddin will serve a three-year term until 2029, joining global industry leaders in providing strategic oversight and policy direction for IATA.
"In this capacity, he will contribute to guiding the association's industry committees and subsidiary bodies on critical issues shaping the future of global aviation," it said.
Nasaruddin also currently serves as chairman of the Association of Asia Pacific Airlines (AAPA) executive committee and is a member of the oneworld governance board.
With more than 30 years of experience in the aviation industry, Nasaruddin began his career with Malaysia Airlines and has since held various leadership roles.
This includes co-chairman of the Asia and Australasia Human Factors Safety Information Network, and innovation garage director in collaboration with Khazanah Nasional Bhd and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd.
Nasaruddin said this appointment carries both privilege and responsibility, particularly at a critical time for global aviation.
"I look forward to working alongside fellow governors to contribute meaningfully to strategic dialogue and policy direction that will strengthen industry resilience, accelerate innovation, and advance a more sustainable and connected future for air transport worldwide," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says G7 support 'irrelevant', claims 'victory' in Iran war
Article:
ISTANBUL: US President Donald Trump has dismissed any support from the G7 as "irrelevant" and claimed that "we have won the war in Iran" in a brief phone interview broadcast by Italian channel La7 on Friday, Anadolu Ajansi reported.
Trump made remarks during a call with the La7 Washington correspondent.
Asked whether he had a message for G7 leaders, Trump said the US had not needed external support.
"We didn't need any support. So, we have won the war. It was a bit irrelevant, irrelevant! I have to go. I'm in an important meeting, but we have won the war in Iran. We didn't need their help," Trump said.
The comments came ahead of the G7 summit scheduled for June 15-17.
Regional tensions that began on Feb 28 after Israeli and US strikes on Iran triggered a cycle of military confrontations, retaliatory attacks and diplomatic disputes.
Iran and Israel also exchanged strikes in recent days before pulling back, highlighting the fragility of a ceasefire and ongoing efforts by regional and international mediators to revive diplomacy and prevent a broader conflict.
– Bernama-Anadolu
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sultan Nazrin urges youth not to let faith diminish the dignity of others
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah has urged young people not to allow anyone to convince them that the dignity of their faith depends on diminishing the dignity of others.
Delivering his royal address at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 today, His Royal Highness said diversity was part of divine wisdom and should inspire people to compete in doing good.
"To the young people present, do not allow anyone to convince you that the dignity of your faith depends on diminishing the dignity of another.
"As the Holy Quran makes abundantly clear, Allah, had He so willed, would have made us all a single community — one religion, one race and one nation — but He chose otherwise, and instructed us instead to compete with one another in goodness," he said.
Sultan Nazrin called on religious leaders to turn the summit into a commitment to mentorship, partnership and practical action.
He said they must create spaces where young people from different faiths and backgrounds could meet as fellow citizens and fellow human beings.
"Let us equip them to speak truthfully, to listen generously and to lead courageously," he said.
His Royal Highness said the future of faith and coexistence among religious traditions would not be secured by one generation alone.
"It will be secured when wisdom and youth walk together, when sacred traditions open hearts, when inherited values are renewed through living action, and when harmony is carried forward not as a fragile slogan but as a shared discipline," he said.
Sultan Nazrin said coexistence had become an act of courage in an age of fragmentation, while "radical hope" had become an act of resistance in an age of cynicism.
He said religious traditions could offer a foundation of peace from which compassion, trust and belonging could grow.
"Our task is not to manage the diversity of this world. It is to help humanity remember how to belong to one another again," he said.
Sultan Nazrin said young people should be offered not only guidance, but also hope and a meaningful role in shaping the future.
Citing a teaching of Prophet Muhammad, he said that even if the Final Hour were upon a person and a sapling was in his hand, he should still plant it.
"That is what we owe the young, not a sermon about the storm, but a seedling pressed into their palm.
"Let us be the ones who handed them a sapling, a reason to believe and a seat at the table," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor strengthens sustainability agenda through RM2bln WTE investment
Article:
KUALA SELANGOR: The Selangor government, through its subsidiary Worldwide Holdings Bhd (Worldwide), has invested approximately RM2 billion to develop three waste-to-energy (WTE) plants as part of a long-term plan to improve sustainable solid waste management in the state.
Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the three plants, located in Jeram and Tanjung Dua Belas, Kuala Langat, will play an important role in managing a large portion of the state's domestic solid waste, which is currently estimated at 7,000 to 8,000 tonnes per day.
According to Worldwide, WTE Plant 1, which has begun operations in Jeram, is capable of processing up to 1,500 tonnes of solid waste per day and generating about 26 megawatts (MW) of electricity, enough to meet the needs of 26,000 households, depending on energy consumption patterns.
"When WTE 2 is completed and fully operational, waste management capacity in Jeram will increase to approximately 3,500 tonnes per day. The remaining 2,000 tonnes will be managed through WTE 3 in Tanjung Dua Belas.
"These three plants are expected to significantly reduce the amount of waste that must be sent to landfills. Of the projected 7,000 to 8,000 tonnes per day, only around 2,500 to 3,000 tonnes of non-combustible (inert) waste will continue to be disposed of in landfills," he told the media after officiating the opening ceremony of WTE Plant 1 in Jeram today.
The event was also attended by the state executive councillor for local government and tourism, Datuk Ng Suee Lim, and Worldwide Holdings group chief executive officer Datin Paduka Norazlina Zakaria.
Commenting on the project's progress, Amirudin said that the WTE 2 is expected to be completed as early as October, or at the latest by November this year, while construction of the WTE 3 in Tanjung Dua Belas has already begun. He said that based on the experience of developing WTE 1 and WTE 2, which commenced in 2023, the construction period for a WTE plant typically takes between two and two-and-a-half years to complete.
"For the project in Jeram, there were several technical challenges, including the need to address buffer zone issues during construction, which caused the implementation period to take slightly longer," he added.
He said the WTE 3 is expected to begin operations by late 2027 or early 2028, thereby completing the state's network of WTE facilities and enhancing waste management efficiency and renewable energy generation.
Amirudin assured that the operations of all WTE plants comply with the strict standards set by the Department of Environment (DOE).
He said that the centralised monitoring system in the plant's control room allows operational data and emissions readings to be transmitted directly to the DOE, ensuring continuous compliance. "We practice a high level of transparency and will continue to ensure that all criteria and standards set by the authorities are fully complied with," he added.
– BERNAMA
TAGS: Selangor WTE, waste to energy, Amirudin Shari, solid waste management, Worldwide Holding
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pasir Gudang chemical leak: Scheduled waste facility owner, supervisor detained
Article:
PASIR GUDANG: The owner and supervisor of a scheduled waste facility operating in an industrial area here were detained today after one of its tanks leaked scheduled waste chemicals that caused yellowish-orange smoke to fill the air yesterday.
The Johor Department of Environment detained the 34-year-old plant owner and a 41-year-old supervisor for alleged breaches of environmental laws.
State DoE director Dr Mohd Famey Yusoff said the arrests were carried out after a thorough investigation into public complaints over the incident, with enforcement teams confirming serious breaches of environmental laws, including failure to comply with scheduled waste management procedures and licence conditions under the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
He said evidence from the site had also been seized as part of ongoing investigations, while air quality monitoring conducted at nearby sensitive locations, including SMK Taman Pasir Putih and SMK Pasir Gudang 3, using a portable multi-gas analyser, detected no hazardous gas parameters as of today.
Dr Famey said swift action involving multiple agencies, including the police, Fire and Rescue Department Hazmat team, and the Pasir Gudang City Council, helped
contain the situation
and prevent wider impact on surrounding communities.
He added there would be zero compromise against any industrial operators who fail to comply with scheduled waste regulations, warning that the toughest legal action would be taken against offenders.
The public can report environmental pollution incidents via the JAS hotline, email, or the department's e-complaint portal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia issues UMA query to Paragon Union after share price falls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd has issued an Unusual Market Activity (UMA) query to Paragon Union Bhd following a sharp fall in the company's share price today.
At 4.41pm, the group's share price slipped by RM1.39, or 29.83 per cent, to RM3.27 from RM4.66 at Thursday's close.
In a Bursa filing, the exchange advised investors to take note of the company's response to the UMA query, which will be posted on Bursa Malaysia's website under company announcements.
Bursa Malaysia said it requested Paragon Union to determine whether there were any undisclosed corporate developments, including those under negotiation or discussion, that could have contributed to the sharp movement in its share price.
The exchange also sought clarification on whether any rumours or reports concerning the group's business and affairs may have influenced the trading activity, and whether the group was aware of any other factors that could have contributed to the sharp movement in its share price.
Additionally, Bursa Malaysia also requested confirmation that Paragon Union had complied with the Main Market Listing Requirements, particularly those relating to immediate disclosure obligations.
Bursa Malaysia added that the contents of the company's response must be endorsed by Paragon Union's board of directors before being submitted through Bursa LINK.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Attractive valuations drive buying, Bursa Malaysia ends higher
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Selected blue-chip counters trading at attractive valuations helped Bursa Malaysia close higher on Friday, despite cautious market sentiment driven by developments in West Asia, volatility in crude oil prices and uncertainty over the global interest rate outlook.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose by 4.10 points or 0.24 per cent to 1,683.63 compared with Thursday's close of 1,679.53.
The key index opened 5.34 points stronger at 1,684.87 earlier today and moved between 1,679.72 and 1,685.39 throughout the session.
Market breadth was negative, with losers leading gainers 533 to 479, while 561 counters were unchanged, 1,130 untraded and 33 suspended.
Turnover fell to 2.79 billion units worth RM2.31 billion from 3.32 billion units worth RM2.88 billion yesterday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said the FBM KLCI moved in a tight range and closed marginally higher on low volume as regional equities ended mostly higher, supported by optimism surrounding potential progress in the United States-Iran relations and a rebound in technology and chipmaking stocks.
"On the domestic front, trading activity remained subdued as investors stayed on the sidelines amid persistent foreign selling and a lack of strong market catalysts.
"At the same time, attractive valuations in selected blue-chip counters may continue to encourage bargain hunting and provide support to the broader market," he told Bernama.
Among the heavyweight counters, Malayan Banking Bhd added six sen to RM10.72, Public Bank Bhd was five sen to RM4.83, Tenaga Nasional Bhd gained 12 sen to RM14.42, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd was nine sen better at RM7.39, and IHH Healthcare Bhd climbed 11 sen to RM8.78.
Among the active stocks, Zetrix AI Bhd was flat at 81 sen, Top Glove Corp Bhd was 2.5 sen lower at 80.5 sen, AirAsia X Bhd added six sen to RM1.20, Capital A Bhd was unchanged at 40 sen, and Dagang NeXchange Bhd perked up 1.5 sen to 36 sen.
Of the top gainers, Nestle (M) Bhd put on RM1.10 sen to RM95.60, Fraser & Neave Holdings garnered 94 sen to RM26.74,  Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd improved 58 sen to RM15.30, and Petronas Dagangan Bhd rose 48 sen to RM18.20.
Among the top losers, Paragon Union Bhd plunged RM1.39 to RM3.27, Batu Kawan Bhd tumbled 96 sen to RM19.84, and Petronas Chemicals Bhd slid 46 sen to RM5.34, while Unisem M Bhd and Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd slipped 18 sen each to RM4.60 and RM20.76, respectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Religious leaders and youth: Advancing coexistence and social harmony
Article:
The following is the full text of Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah's special address at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre on Friday.
1.
We meet in a world that is volatile, uncertain, complex and ambiguous; a world whose geopolitical stakes, conflicts, and fractures have been well mapped by those who study such things. What I want to ask today is a different question: what does it take, on the ground, in communities, in relationships, to make peace real?
I want to speak about the people inheriting this world, young people, and about the difference between simply declaring peace and doing the hard work of building it.
2.
Let me begin not with a statistic, but with a face, because every statistic we discuss in this gathering has a face and a story.
3.
Picture a 17-year-old. She has never set foot in a hall like this. She will never read the communique we issue at the close of this summit.
But tonight, in a room lit only by the glow of a screen, she is being spoken to, patiently, persuasively, and persistently, by a voice telling her that the world is divided into 'them' and 'us'; that her neighbour is her enemy; that her faith demands her rage.
4.
That voice is not a scholar. It is not an imam, a priest, a rabbi, or a monk.
It is an algorithm.
And that algorithm is being deployed for profit by people who profess the very faith they are weaponising.
5.
AI-generated disinformation, dressed up in the vocabulary of grievance, now reaches hundreds of millions of young people at a scale and speed no sermon ever could.
The argument for division is being won by voices we do not control, on platforms we do not own, in a language we have been too slow to learn.
That is why I wish to speak today not about us, but about her, and the hundreds of millions like her.
6.
Climate anxiety, conflict and displacement, economic insecurity, digital polarisation, the erosion of institutional trust: these converge and reinforce one another in ways that resist simplistic remedies.
And beneath them lies a quieter crisis: one of meaning, belonging, and trust.
Young people today are asking fundamental questions:
Where do I belong?
Who can I trust?
What values still matter?
What future are we building together?
7.
There are close to 1.8 billion young people on this earth; the largest generation in all human history.
Our own ummah is younger still: the median age of Muslims worldwide is around 24, against a global median of roughly 30, making Muslims the youngest of all faith communities on earth.
8.
The young do not merely inherit our conflicts; they bear them most heavily, and they are too often denied any hand in resolving them.
In 2015, the United Nations Security Council acknowledged this when it adopted Resolution 2250 on Youth, Peace and Security, recognising that young people are not merely the victims of conflict, but among its most effective resolvers.
The verdict of the decade since is sobering.
We have spent a great deal of time talking about young people, and far too little time listening to them and sharing power with them.
The phrase the young peacebuilders themselves now use is exact, and it should sting us: they ask to be treated as co-creators, not consultants.
I put it to this assembly that faith leaders are uniquely placed to close that gap.
9.
The violent extremist and the religious teacher are competing for the same young hearts, in the same sacred vocabulary.
The extremist does not approach the young with a dry political manifesto. He comes clothed in scripture, quoting the very verses we quote.
He invokes the same hunger for meaning, the same clarity of purpose, the same ache for dignity, and with great skill bends it away from mercy and towards grievance.
He offers the sense of belonging that comes from having an imagined enemy.
If all we offer in return is a sermon young people find remote, in a language they have stopped speaking, delivered inside a building they have stopped entering, then we have come armed with a manuscript to a contest being fought on iPhones.
10.
Last month, Pope Leo XIV issued his encyclical Magnifica Humanitas on safeguarding the human person in the age of artificial intelligence.
He cautioned that access to limitless information must never be confused with wisdom; that the algorithm can inform a young mind, but it cannot form a young conscience.
On this, a Catholic pontiff and a Muslim imam can speak with one voice: the screen can deliver information, but only a human being can deliver meaning.
11.
If we surrender the work of belonging to the machine and its manipulators, we should not wonder where our young have gone.
And so, the difficult question for every leader in this hall must be this:
Do the young people of your community experience your tradition as a living wellspring of belonging, or as an inheritance they are leaving behind?
12.
We speak today as representatives of institutions that have, for centuries, served as anchors of meaning, belonging, and the moral life.
But we must be honest: these anchors have come loose.
The 2026 Edelman Barometer paints a sobering picture.
Seven in 10 people globally are unwilling or hesitant to trust someone with different values or beliefs.
Only one in three believes the next generation will be better off.
Religious institutions are not exempt from this decline, and we do ourselves no favours by pretending otherwise.
13.
This is where religious leadership has a vital role to play.
At its best, religion teaches us to look beyond the self.
It reminds us that human beings are not merely consumers, competitors, or avatars in a digital crowd, but persons of dignity and moral worth.
14.
To engage the young, religious leadership must be both rooted and responsive. Rooted because communities need the wisdom, discipline and moral depth that tradition provides. Responsive because each generation encounters new realities and asks new questions. The task is not to dilute faith for our youth, nor to abandon inherited wisdom, but to bring that wisdom into living conversation with the conditions of the present world.
15.
We often speak of young people as the future. But let us think about what this quietly implies: that their time has not yet come, that the present moment belongs to those already holding authority. That framing, however well-meaning, is one of the most common mistakes institutions make.
16.
For young people are the present. They are organising, innovating, mobilising movements, and reshaping public discourse in the digital and physical spaces we share. In many ways, they are ahead of us, more connected across borders, more at ease with difference than their elders ever were. Our task should be to nurture their idealism, to keep it from souring into cynicism or being captured by those who would turn its energy to ruinous ends.
17.
The Prophet Muhammad, sallallahu 'alaiyhi wassalam, taught us that the best people are those most beneficial to others.
He did not say the most learned, the most powerful, the eldest, or the most senior.
By that measure, a generation hungry to be of use is not our anxiety to be contained. It is our hope to be unleashed.
18.
The question is not whether young people are ready to participate. They have been participating — despite us, around us, and often without us.
The question is whether institutions are ready to treat the young as co-creators, partners, and architects of the societies they are already shaping.
19.
Yet we must be honest about the world in which they have come of age. This is a generation growing up amid extraordinary noise. Although they are more connected than any generation before them, many young people experience deep loneliness. They have unprecedented access to knowledge, and yet must navigate misinformation, cynicism, hatred, and distortion.
They encounter difference every day, but often in digital spaces designed to reward outrage and bullying. In such an environment, coexistence can no longer be assumed. It must be taught, practised, protected, and renewed.
20.
Southeast Asia has often been described as a fertile garden in which many strains of spirituality have flowered side by side. In Malaysia, we understand that harmony is not uniformity. Our diversity is our strength and our responsibility.
21.
The Charter of Madinah understood this. It was a moral operating system for coexistence in dignity and shared responsibility.
22.
This wisdom is shared across traditions: the Quran, the Gospel, the Torah, Buddhism, Hinduism, Confucianism — all affirm the dignity of the other.
23.
Let me return to that 17-year-old.
24.
We will not reach her with a communique. We will reach her only through lived example and trusted voices close to her world.
25.
Let me offer four commitments.
26.
First, give the young real authority.
27.
Second, meet them where they are.
28.
Third, make encounters ordinary, not exceptional.
29.
Fourth, address grievance, not only symptoms.
30.
To religious leaders: build mentorship and partnership.
31.
To the young: do not let anyone reduce your faith into hostility toward others.
32.
The future will be secured when wisdom and youth walk together.
33.
In an age of fragmentation, coexistence is courage. In an age of cynicism, hope is resistance. In an age of noise, Sakinah is the gift we must protect.
34.
If the Final Hour arrives and we hold a sapling, we must still plant it. That is what we owe the young: not only words, but action, trust, and a place at the table.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar says religious leaders key in addressing moral crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the role of religious leaders had become increasingly important in upholding humanitarian values amid a deepening global moral crisis.
"The role of religious leaders remains more relevant than ever because they represent the voice of conscience, reason, patience and tolerance — qualities that are in short supply in today's world," he said at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 today.
Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, was present to grace the summit held at the KLCC Convention Centre.
Also present were Muslim World League Secretary-General Sheikh Dr Mohammad Al-Issa, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Datuk Dr Zulkifli Hasan, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir, and Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.
Anwar said the summit came at an important time as the world grappled with crises marked by volatility, uncertainty, complexity and ambiguity.
More alarmingly, he said, religion — which should be a source of peace, tranquillity, prosperity and cohesion — was often politicised and manipulated to fuel hostility.
"This is also a challenge to religious leaders. Having said and given all the accolades about your role, your contributions, your sacrifice, please do acknowledge the fact that there have been major transgressions in the name of religion.
"We are supposed to promote peace, but promote discord instead; supposed to promote tolerance and justice, but condone violence and injustice," he said.
Anwar said the Charter of Madinah remained a guiding paradigm in the Muslim experience, laying the foundation for a state that recognised religious and racial diversity.
He said Prophet Muhammad did not marginalise any group, but instead established a social contract that preserved the dignity of every individual.
Anwar said the peace Malaysia enjoyed today was proof that people of different faiths could live together in harmony.
He said this harmony stemmed from openness to interaction and dialogue, citing his attendance at a Wesak Day gathering last month, his participation in preparatory arrangements for Thaipusam, and the country's celebration of Christmas.
"That does not in any way deter us from remaining steadfast practising Muslims, proud to be great Muslim believers.
"Our strength lies not in uniformity, but in our capacity to manage differences fairly and justly," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz urges BN machinery to campaign with discipline and decorum
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has urged the coalition's machinery to campaign with discipline and decorum ahead of the 16th Johor state election on July 11.
In a statement, he called on all party members to make full use of the campaign period to present their track record, plans and direction in a mature and orderly manner.
"I call on the entire BN Johor machinery to campaign with full order and civility," he said.
He also appealed directly to Johor voters, urging them to make fair and rational assessments at the ballot box.
"Evaluate based on track record, stability, achievements and the ability to continue bringing Johor forward," he said.
Onn Hafiz said he is confident the state remained on a strong trajectory and called on voters to support continued stability and progress.
"Johor has been on a positive path. With the support of the people, we will continue to safeguard stability and pursue progress for the future of the state and the people of Johor," he said.
He also expressed appreciation to the Election Commission for its preparations in conducting the democratic process in accordance with legal provisions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman injured in off-duty accident eligible for Perkeso's Lindung 2/7 scheme
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has confirmed that a woman who sustained serious injuries, including a fractured shoulder and leg, in an accident after a hiking activity at Bukit Chanang near Keningau, is eligible to receive benefits under the Lindung 24/7 (Lindung 24 Jam) scheme.
In a statement today, Perkeso said Brissty Vidalyn Felicianus, 33, who is an active contributor under the scheme, was involved in an accident on June 2 when the four-wheel-drive vehicle she was travelling in veered into a ravine while descending from the hiking starting point.
"As a result of the accident, Brissty suffered a fractured right shoulder and right leg, minor injuries on the left side of her head, and other parts of her body. She is reportedly receiving treatment at Keningau Hospital.
"As an active contributor, she is eligible for temporary disability benefits, which serve as income replacement during her certified medical leave by a medical officer," it said.
Perkeso said Brissty has been issued a medical leave certificate until June 15 and is scheduled to undergo surgery to insert metal implants to treat her fractures.
"Perkeso's Prihatin Squad visited the victim at her home on June 9 to obtain the necessary information. We will continue to monitor her condition and are ready to provide rehabilitation treatment to ensure she can return to work.
"The incident involving the Keningau Community Rehabilitation Organisation employee showed that accidents can occur at any time and are not limited to the workplace," it said.
Perkeso said it has received several non-work-related accident cases within days after the implementation of the Lindung 24/7 scheme, further reinforcing the need for a more comprehensive protection.
"This proves that social protection should not be limited solely to working hours. As such, comprehensive social security coverage is essential to prevent workers from facing financial hardship when disaster strikes," it said.
The non-work-related accident scheme was introduced to ensure workers in Malaysia have continuous protection in the event of unforeseen incidents that may affect income, health, and family well-being.
Perkeso said through this initiative, contributors who are injured outside working hours remain protected by a social safety net that supports their recovery and helps them resume their lives in a more structured manner.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Auction set for seized Jamal assets in MP Teresa Kok costs dispute
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The 14 household items seized from the home of Sungai Besar Umno chief Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos will be auctioned on June 22 to recover RM66,061.82 in unpaid legal costs owed to Seputeh member of parliament (MP) Teresa Kok.
Kok's lawyer, Datuk S.N. Nair, said the High Court disposed of both interpleader claims before senior assistant registrar Qasiratul Jannah Usmani Othman during a hearing today.
Sankara said one claimant, Mohd Razali Kamin, who said he owned several seized items, filed a notice of discontinuance and was ordered to pay RM2,000 in costs.
"The interpleader claim by Muhammad Amin Daniel Jamal (Jamal's son) was dismissed with costs of RM8,000.
"Accordingly, there is no longer any impediment to the execution proceedings.
"The auction of the seized goods has been fixed for June 22 at 10am," he said.
Sankara said the judgment creditor was represented by Maniggandan Gopalan and Emeli Diyana Razali, while the claimants were represented by Nasser Yusof.
On Jan 28, a court assistant registrar ordered a forced entry at Jamal's residence in Ampang Jaya, resulting in the seizure of 14 household items to recover the outstanding sum.
The action stems from a final High Court judgment obtained by Kok against Jamal in a defamation suit in 2022 that remains unsatisfied.
On July 26, 2022, Kok won the suit over Jamal's defamatory remarks concerning the alleged misuse of Yayasan Warisan Anak Selangor funds during a press conference on March 8, 2017. She filed the suit on April 6, 2017.
Judicial Commissioner Datuk Mohd Arief Emran Arifin ordered Jamal to pay RM300,000 in general, aggravated and exemplary damages, as well as RM50,000 in costs.
On March 29, 2023, the Court of Appeal granted Jamal a conditional stay, requiring him to deposit RM300,000 in damages into Kok's solicitors' account, which he did.
A separate Court of Appeal panel upheld the High Court ruling on March 27, 2024. The Federal Court subsequently dismissed Jamal's application for leave to appeal on July 30, 2024.
Kok obtained the writ of seizure and sale after Jamal failed to pay the RM50,000 in legal costs.
Sankara said Jamal had settled the damages award, but the legal costs remained unpaid, prompting enforcement action at his residence.
A previous attempt to execute the order on Oct 9, 2024, failed because no one was at home.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 1.88 million Malaysians benefit from mySalam; RM1.42 billion paid out
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Some 1.88 million Malaysians have benefited from the mySalam health protection scheme, with claims amounting to RM1.42 billion disbursed between 2019 and Dec 31 last year.
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was presented at today's cabinet meeting, which also reviewed the programme's performance under the purview of the Finance Ministry.
He said mySalam, which was launched in 2019 with an allocation of RM2.37 billion, was introduced to provide health protection coverage for eligible Malaysians.
Eligible recipients include beneficiaries of the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah and their spouses, up to the age of 65.
"For hospitalisation benefits, 1.294 million recipients received a total of RM394.87 million.
"Meanwhile, 89,085 recipients benefited from the critical illness coverage, with payouts totalling RM712 million, while 13,128 recipients received RM65.257 million for medical devices," he said at his weekly post-cabinet meeting press briefing.
The scheme offers three main benefits, one of which is hospitalisation payments of RM50 per day for up to 14 days, or a maximum of RM700 annually.
It also provides a one-off payment of up to RM8,000 for individuals diagnosed with any of the 50 listed critical illnesses, as well as medical device claims of up to RM30,000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sultan Nazrin warns AI disinformation fuelling youth division
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, has warned that artificial intelligence (AI)-generated disinformation is fuelling division among youths at a scale and speed that traditional religious institutions are struggling to match.
Delivering a royal address at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 today, His Royal Highness said extremist narratives were reaching young people through digital platforms, often by exploiting grievances and religious language.
"AI-generated disinformation, dressed up in the vocabulary of grievance, now reaches hundreds of millions of young people at a scale and speed no sermon ever could.
"The argument for division is being won by voices we do not control, on platforms we do not own, in a language we have been too slow to learn," he said at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre today.
Present were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Muslim World League Secretary-General Sheikh Dr Mohammad Al-Issa, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir and Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.
Also present were delegations representing religious leaders from around the world, as well as members of diplomatic missions.
Sultan Nazrin said a young person today could be influenced not by scholars or religious leaders, but by algorithms designed to draw them into division and anger.
"That voice is not a scholar. It is not an imam, a priest, a rabbi or a monk. It is an algorithm. And that algorithm is being deployed for profit by people who profess the very faith they are weaponising," he said.
He said violent extremists and religious teachers were competing for the same young hearts, using the same sacred vocabulary.
"If all we offer in return is a sermon young people find remote, in a language they have stopped speaking, delivered inside a building they have stopped entering, then we have come armed with a manuscript to a contest being fought on iPhones," he added.
Sultan Nazrin said religious leadership must be both rooted and responsive in engaging young people, as communities needed the wisdom, discipline and moral depth that tradition provides.
He said the task was not to dilute faith for the young or abandon inherited wisdom, but to bring that wisdom into conversation with the conditions of the present world.
"We often speak of young people as the future. But let us think about what this quietly implies; that their time has not yet come, that the present moment belongs to those already holding authority.
"That framing, however well-meaning, is one of the most common mistakes institutions make," his Royal Highness added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mesiniaga wins RM43.28mil contract from Maybank unit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mesiniaga Bhd has secured a RM43.28 million contract from Maybank Shared Services Sdn Bhd for the renewal of a licence subscription agreement over a three-year period ending May 31, 2029.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the information technology services provider said the contract was awarded on June 11 and does not include an automatic renewal provision.
Mesiniaga said the contract is expected to contribute to its earnings and net assets from the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026 until the completion of the agreement.
The company added that risk management measures are in place to address factors such as customer delivery timelines.
This is the second contract Mesiniaga has secured from Maybank Shared Services this year.
In April, the company won a RM51.64 million contract for cloud infrastructure software subscriptions and support services.
Shares of Mesiniaga were unchanged at RM1.23 at Friday's noon break, valuing the group at RM74.3 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 36 questioned over Jaslinda's 14-day disappearance on Gunung Batu Putih
Article:
IPOH: Police have recorded statements from 36 individuals regarding the disappearance of climber Jaslinda Saludin, who was found safe last Saturday, 14 days after she went missing on Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah.
Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said those who gave statements included Jaslinda, her husband, Haszman Othman, 61, and friends involved in the climbing activity.
He said a statement was also taken from an individual who allegedly uploaded defamatory content against the Tok Batin of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, regarding Jaslinda's discovery.
"The man has been remanded for four days starting today to assist in the investigation into the defamation case.
"The case involving Jaslinda is being investigated under Section 500 of the Penal Code, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955," he said at a press conference at the Senior Police Officers' Mess in Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah here today.
The press conference was held after he witnessed the handover ceremony of the Perak Commercial Crime Investigation Department head.
Last Saturday, Jaslinda, 49, was found safe by residents of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu after going missing on May 24 while climbing Gunung Batu Putih.
Previously, state Commercial Crime Investigation Department deputy head (investigation/legal) Superintendent Mohd Ridzuan Nor Ihsan was appointed acting head of the department, replacing Assistant Commissioner Mohd Fahmi Visuvanathan Abdullah, who retires on June 18.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold steadies, but heads for second straight weekly loss
Article:
NEW YORK: Gold steadied on Friday as oil fell on rising hopes for a peace deal between Iran and the United States, but prices remained on track for a second straight weekly decline due to expectations of higher interest rates.
Spot gold was little changed at US$4,217.95 per ounce, as of 0843 GMT, but was down more than two per cent for the week.
US gold futures rose three per cent to US$4,238.50.
"Gold prices are moving in line with statements from the US President, but the overriding influence remains inflation, with markets expecting rate hikes from the ECB and also anticipating that the Fed will follow," said Quantitative Commodity Research analyst Peter Fertig.
"Gold's price move will depend on how steep the increase in inflation rate is. If we see a further acceleration in the coming months, the US$4,000 handle could be broken to the downside."
US President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran could be signed as soon as this weekend, even as Tehran said it had not made a final decision on a pact. Oil prices extended losses from the previous session on the news.
Gold has been under strain since the Iran conflict began, with worries that surging energy costs may drive inflation higher, reinforcing expectations that central banks will keep interest rates elevated, thereby increasing the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion.
ANZ lowered its year-end price target for gold by US$400 to US$5,200 to reflect recent price volatility.
Data this week showed that US producer prices increased more than expected in May, while consumer inflation jumped above four per cent in May for the first time in three years.
Focus is also on the Federal Reserve's policy-setting committee that meets on June 16-17. It is the first policy meeting led by new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.
Among other metals, spot silver fell 0.7 per cent to US$66.91 per ounce, and platinum was flat at US$1,719.92, with both metals headed for a weekly loss.
Palladium rose 3.1 per cent to US$1,308.39, and headed for a weekly gain.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MIPP hails 'Reset' convention as platform for national renewal
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) has described the "Reset" convention tomorrow as an important platform for constructive discussions on national renewal, governance, economic resilience and Malaysia's future.
While extending his congratulations and best wishes to Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin on the inaugural convention, MIPP president P. Punithan said the convention provided a timely opportunity for stakeholders to exchange ideas and deliberate on issues affecting the nation's development and future direction.
"The 'Reset' Convention is an important platform for constructive discussions on national renewal, governance, economic resilience and Malaysia's future.
"May the convention inspire fresh ideas, strengthen unity among Malaysians and contribute meaningfully towards building a stronger, more prosperous and inclusive nation," he said.
Punithan also expressed hope that 'Reset' leaders, members and supporters would continue to stand behind the aspirations of Perikatan Nasional while working towards building broader support across communities.
He said such efforts were important in advancing the coalition's goal of forming the federal government and ensuring a better future for all Malaysians.
The convention, which will be held in Tanah Merah, is expected to serve as a platform for exchanging views and formulating strategies on key national issues, including governance, economic sustainability and social cohesion.
It is expected to gather political leaders, professionals and supporters to discuss issues related to governance, economic resilience and the country's future direction.
It was reported today that about 10,000 people are expected to
attend the event
which will be officiated by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
Hamzah, the former opposition leader, launched the "Reset" movement on Feb 14, a day after Bersatu sacked him amid a leadership tussle with party president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NGO seeks public help to sustain schools for mentally handicapped students
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: For more than 200 mentally disabled students, Wisma Harapan is more than just a school.
The centres, run by the Selangor and Federal Territory Association for the Mentally Handicapped (SAMH) in Klang and Kuala Lumpur, are deeply ingrained in their daily routines, and keeping them operating costs RM150,000 a month.
With only 10 days left before its once-every-three-years charity dinner, tables remain largely unsold and corporate sponsorships are slowing, leaving the welfare of students and more than 45 staff hanging in the balance.
"Sponsorships are slow, and companies are citing economic uncertainties arising from the crisis in the Middle East.
"This will affect us badly because without public donations, the coming months and years will be very difficult for us," said SAMH organising chairperson and deputy president, Tan Sri Hasmah Abdullah.
The 62nd Anniversary Charity Dinner, SAMH's primary fundraising event and financial lifeline, is scheduled for June 20 at Dorsett Grand Subang, with the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, attending as the royal guest of honour.
The association hopes to raise RM500,000 to support its annual operating budget of RM1.7 million.
Unlike government grants, which only cover student-specific needs, SAMH must fully fund the operational costs of both schools, requiring RM500,000 every three months just to keep them running.
Falling short of its fundraising target would mean drawing on dwindling reserves or relying on smaller fundraising initiatives such as food fairs and charity runs.
Hasmah urged those unable to attend the dinner to contribute through direct donations.
"Even if they do not wish to attend the dinner, we hope they will still donate to us," she said.
Tables are priced at RM15,000, RM10,000 and RM5,000, with each table accommodating up to 10 guests.
The evening will feature an eight-course Chinese dinner, a performance by the police combo band and a showcase by SAMH students.
"For many of our students, this is a lifelong commitment because they are not able to work independently outside," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wisma Harapan lifeline at risk as funds run dry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: For 62 years, the Selangor and Federal Territory Association for the Mentally Handicapped (SAMH) has provided life-changing education and vocational training through its Wisma Harapan schools in Klang and Kuala Lumpur.
The centre supports students with autism, Down's syndrome, ADHD and dyspraxia, with programmes ranging from early childhood intervention to adult vocational placements.
For families who have walked through its doors, the impact goes far beyond the classroom.
Dila Hassan said her 34-year-old son, Muhammad Azim Hamzah, who has autism, expresses his deep attachment to the school in his own unique way.
When school holidays arrive, Azim does not say he misses class.
Instead, he watches old videos of Wisma Harapan on YouTube, pointing out teachers and friends by name and drawing the school's familiar corridors from memory.
"That's how he shows us, in his own unique way of communicating," she said.
Another parent, Yunieza Zailan, said she enrolled her 13-year-old son, Rayqal Hakimi, who has ADHD, hoping for a more personalised learning experience.
"Ever since he joined, he has developed better social skills and can now maintain a conversation, something he struggled with before," she said.
The foundation built at SAMH has also enabled older students to transition into the workforce.
After completing the vocational training programme, D. Preeya Nanthini, who has dyspraxia, worked at a kindergarten in Bangsar before securing employment at the PJ EcoRecycling Centre in Petaling Jaya.
Her mother, Dharshini Ganeson, said she credits much of that development to the dedicated training provided by the association.
However, this vital community lifeline is now at serious financial risk.
While the Welfare Department provides limited assistance, including student allowances and small meal and utility grants, SAMH's RM1.7 million annual operating budget is largely self-funded through public donations.
Corporate donors, foundations such as the Malaysian Community Education Foundation, and triennial fundraising gala dinners form the financial backbone of the association.
Because government grants cover only student-specific needs, the association relies heavily on public generosity to fund teacher salaries, facility maintenance and operational costs.
SAMH manager M. Buwanesri said the financial trajectory is unsustainable.
"Most of the year, we operate at a deficit. We are surviving on savings built up over the years, but that can only last so long," she said.
For families relying on Wisma Harapan, the centre remains an irreplaceable sanctuary where individuals with special needs discover their potential and learn who they are.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM directs ministries to gather input on foreign workers manpower
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed the relevant ministries to obtain detailed input from industries and sectors on foreign manpower requirements.
Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said this followed applications from several sectors seeking foreign labour, including the restaurant and construction industries.
"The cabinet meeting today discussed applications from several sectors for foreign labour.
"Given that there is currently a national policy on the cap on the number of foreign workers, the prime minister has instructed ministries such as the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, the Plantation and Commodities Ministry and the Works Ministry to obtain detailed information from the industries and sectors under their respective purview.
"This is to provide a comprehensive and more detailed account of manpower needs, including foreign labour requirements, for further consideration at the upcoming cabinet meeting," he said in his weekly post-cabinet meeting press briefing.
Under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), the government
aims to reduce
the proportion of foreign workers in the workforce to 10 per cent by 2030, down from around 15 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM600,000 needed to demolish illegal Rohingya settlement in Sungai Tekali
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RM600,000 is needed to demolish a four-storey illegal structure allegedly built by the Rohingya community in Sungai Tekali, Hulu Langat, Selangor.
Hulu Langat member of parliament Mohd Sany Hamzan said a proposal for the demolition had been submitted to the Selangor Economic Action Council and was awaiting funding from the state government.
"Once the allocation is approved, the demolition operation will be carried out by the relevant agencies, including the District and Land Office, Kajang Municipal Council and other district-level agencies," he told Harian Metro today.
He said most members of the Rohingya community had already left the area, with only three families believed to remain.
"I have been informed that at least three families are still living there, but the rest have already moved out," he said.
He described issues involving the Rohingya community as highly complex and challenging.
Sany said the government, together with the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR), needed to find a long-term solution to address the Rohingya refugee issue in Malaysia.
He said resolving the matter was not straightforward, adding that its implications extended beyond Malaysia alone.
"We can detain them and place them in immigration detention centres. However, the cost of maintaining one detainee is RM90 per day.
"We also cannot simply send them back to their country of origin. If they are returned, they could face death.
"It is not the same as dealing with illegal immigrants from Indonesia, Bangladesh, Vietnam or other countries," he said.
He said proposals to relocate Rohingya refugees in Malaysia to Bhasan Char, a remote island in Bangladesh, would not be easy to implement.
"In my view, this is not a simple issue. Any move to send them there would require the consent of the United Nations or UNHCR.
"This is because issues relating to their welfare and basic living needs are extremely important," he said.
He said issues linked to the Rohingya community, including crime and public health concerns, must be addressed comprehensively.
Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri
Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay
was reported as saying that the Rohingya refugee issue in Malaysia required a holistic approach involving multiple agencies and relevant countries, rather than relying solely on police enforcement.
Ayob Khan said that, according to UNHCR statistics as of April 2026, there were 219,472 UNHCR card holders in Malaysia, of whom 197,591 were Myanmar nationals.
Of that number, 128,200 were from the Rohingya community.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri
Anwar Ibrahim
earlier said the government will take action against Rohingya refugees found violating the country's laws and regulations, including matters involving businesses and the construction of buildings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mi Technovation executive director Heng Kok Lin retires
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mi Technovation Bhd has announced the retirement of executive director Heng Kok Lin effective today.
Heng, 63, is a Singaporean national who has served on the group's board as an executive director, according to the company's filing with Bursa Malaysia.
He holds a Master's degree in strategic marketing from the University of Wollongong, Australia.
Heng brings more than 33 years of experience in the electronics and semiconductor industries, with expertise spanning printed circuit board assembly as well as semiconductor backend equipment and materials.
The company said Heng has no family relationship with any director or major shareholder of MiT and no conflict of interest with the group.
As at the date of his retirement, Heng held a direct interest in 2.46 million MiT shares, representing a 0.278 per cent stake in the company.
At the time of writing, Mi Technovation's shares were down four sen or 0.83 per cent to RM4.76, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM4.29 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi says Lee should have raised concerns within PKR
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has expressed disappointment over Petaling Jaya member of parliament Lee Chean Chung's failure to use the party's internal channels to voice his views and concerns on the issues he raised.
Fahmi, who is also communications minister, said Lee should have used party platforms to express his views and concerns.
"In PKR's Central Leadership Council, there are many leaders who often hold differing opinions. One of them is Elizabeth Wong.
"During council meetings, she often expressed views that differed greatly from those of the party president and other leaders.
"Yet she used that platform to ensure that, in her view, the party remained consistent and on the right track.
"He should have done the same," he said during his weekly press conference today.
Fahmi said Lee had not attended Central Leadership Council meetings for quite some time.
"Wong was, at one point, associated with a so-called different faction within the party, but she continued to attend meetings and remained committed to expressing her views based on principle.
"Therefore, I am disappointed by his actions," he said.
Fahmi added that Lee also owed an explanation to those who voted for him, particularly party members.
Earlier today, Lee resigned from the party's Central Leadership Council, citing
dissatisfaction
with the party leadership and its handling of issues affecting elected representatives.
His resignation came after 18 years with PKR and eight years serving in positions at the party's central level.
However, Lee said he would remain a PKR member and continue serving as the Petaling Jaya member of parliament until the current parliamentary term ends.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court clears way for auction of Jamal Yunos’ seized assets after failure to pay Theresa Kok
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — A total of 14 movable properties seized from the residence of Sungai Besar Umno division chief Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos will be auctioned on June 22 after he failed to pay an outstanding judgment amounting to RM66,061.85 to Seputeh Member of Parliament Teresa Kok, in a defamation suit case.
Lawyer Datuk SN Nair, who represented Kok, said the High Court today dismissed the interpleader claim application submitted by Jamal’s son, Muhammad Amin Daniel, who claimed that the seized items belonged to him.
“Muhammad Amin Daniel’s application was dismissed with costs of RM8,000. However, another individual named Mohd Razali Kamin has filed a notice of withdrawal of the interpleader claim and he was ordered to pay costs of RM2,000.
“Therefore, there is no longer any obstacle to the proceedings for the execution of the judgment. The auction of the seized goods is set for June 22, 2026 at 10am,” he said when contacted after the proceedings before senior assistant registrar Qasiratul Jannah Usmani Othman which took place via video conference today.
On January 28, the senior assistant registrar together with bailiffs, lawyers and police officers went to Jamal’s residence in Ampang Jaya, Selangor, to execute the writ of seizure and sale against the 14 movable properties in question.
The execution of the writ of seizure and sale by the court bailiff was to recover the outstanding judgment amount, which is the cost that should have been paid to Kok in the suit case, but has not yet been paid.
On July 30, 2024, the Federal Court dismissed Jamal’s application for leave to appeal against the High Court’s decision ordering him to pay RM300,000 in damages to Kok. Jamal paid the damages.
On July 26 2022, Judge Datuk Mohd Arief Emran Arifin ordered Jamal to pay damages of RM300,000 and costs of RM50,000 to Kok.
Kok filed the suit on April 6, 2017, claiming that Jamal had issued defamatory statements against her regarding the Yawas funds during a press conference on March 8 of the same year.
Through the statement of claim, Kok claimed that the defamatory statements were spread through print and electronic media, including through Jamal’s Facebook account, which gave the impression that she had misused state government funds for personal gain and was an unethical person. — Bernama

Title: Anwar: Malaysia’s strength lies in managing differences fairly, religion must promote peace
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Malaysia’s strength lies not in uniformity but in its ability to manage differences fairly and justly, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Speaking at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 here, Anwar said the country’s experience as a multi-racial and multi-religious nation demonstrates that people of different faiths and backgrounds can coexist peacefully while remaining committed to their respective beliefs.
The Prime Minister noted that while Islam is the religion of the federation as enshrined in the Federal Constitution, Malaysia remains a multi-racial nation where people exercise their rights and responsibilities with mutual respect and understanding.
“This is a multi-racial country; we exercise our rights and our duties with respect and deference. We want to ensure that every single member of our society, every single citizen, feels safe and equally protected.
“Therefore, our strength lies not in uniformity, but in our capacity to manage differences fairly and justly. We believe that this spirit, if consistently followed, will be the example for humanity at large,” he said.
Anwar also urged religious leaders to continue using their moral authority and influence to promote peace and justice, saying their role has become increasingly important amid growing global conflicts and humanitarian crises.
He stressed that the world was facing mounting challenges, demanding stronger moral leadership alongside a greater commitment to humanity and ethical values.
Anwar said such developments only underscored the growing importance of religious leaders in advocating reason, tolerance and moral responsibility in an increasingly uncertain world.
“Multilateralism is replaced by unilateralism. Peace is replaced by the propensity to continue unabated wars. The plight of the poor and the marginalised has been largely ignored.
“The role of religious leaders remains more relevant than ever because you represent the voice of conscience, of reason, of patience and of tolerance, which is in deficit in today’s world. Some call it post-normal times,” he said.
At the same time, Anwar warned that religion, which ought to be a wellspring of peace, tranquillity, prosperity and cohesion, was increasingly being politicised and manipulated to fuel hostility.
“Religion is supposed to promote peace but promotes discord instead. It is supposed to promote tolerance and justice but condones violence and injustice,” he said.
The Prime Minister also cited the continuing violence in Gaza and Lebanon as examples of the failure to uphold humanity and justice, noting that ceasefires were often violated shortly after being announced.
He said wars involving the wanton killing of women and children were increasingly portrayed as nothing unusual, while younger generations were being exposed to scenes of violence, destruction and human indignity.
“Ceasefires are declared only to be broken even before the ink has dried. Bombardments are carried out even as negotiations are being held,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi disappointed as Lee Chean Chung quits PKR council, says voters deserve answers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today expressed disappointment over the resignation of Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung from the party’s central leadership council, saying that he owed an explanation to his voters and party members.
Fahmi said he had known Lee for many years and believed the MP should have used the party’s internal platforms to raise any concerns rather than leaving the party.
“I am disappointed with the decision he has taken. Many people voted for Lee Chean Chung and he owes an explanation to his constituents as well as members of the party,” he told reporters after the Cabinet meeting during the communications ministry’s weekly press conference here today.
He said differing views within PKR were not uncommon and pointed to senior party leader Elizabeth Wong, who is also the former member of Selangor State Executive Council as an example of someone who regularly expressed views that differed from the party leadership while continuing to engage through party channels.
Fahmi, who is also the communications minister, added that Lee had not attended central leadership council meetings for some time and should have used those avenues to voice his concerns.
“Within the MPP, there are many leaders who do not necessarily share the same views. Elizabeth Wong has often expressed opinions that differ significantly from those of the party leadership, but she continues to use the available platform to ensure the party remains on the right track.
“Lee should have done the same. The platform existed for him to raise his views and concerns,” he said.
Fahmi also stressed that party leaders remained open to discussions and engagement.
He added that party members and leaders were always willing to engage on matters affecting the party’s direction and policies.
“The door has always been open for discussion. In fact, he has been in contact with me before, but he never raised this particular issue,” he said.
“If there is a willingness to find a solution, there is always a way forward,” he said.
After 18 years, Lee has stepped down from the party’s Central Leadership Council, citing concerns over the party’s current direction and internal decision-making processes.
However, he also confirmed that he will remain a PKR member and continue serving as a Member of Parliament until Parliament is dissolved.
“PKR today is no longer the PKR I once knew,” he said in a Facebook post announcing his decision.
He added that he could no longer continue within the party’s leadership structure and would instead focus on his responsibilities as a backbench MP.
Lee also reaffirmed his commitment to parliamentary duties, including supporting institutional reform legislation expected to be tabled in June.
In his statement, he said he had sought clarification from the party regarding recent administrative matters affecting constituency work and public programmes, but claimed that no formal explanation had been provided.
He added that these developments had made it increasingly difficult for him to continue operating within the current leadership framework.

Title: Hospitalisation, critical illness and medical device aid among RM1.42b MySalam payouts to Malaysians, says minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said a total of 1.88 million Malaysians have benefited from the MySalam health protection scheme since its introduction in 2019, with claims amounting to RM1.423 billion as at December 31, 2025.
He said eligible recipients comprise beneficiaries of the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) programme and their spouses aged up to 65.
“Beneficiaries are entitled to three forms of assistance.
“The first is a hospitalisation benefit of RM50 per day for up to 14 days annually, with a maximum annual payment of RM700,” he said during the ministry’s weekly press conference here today.
The second benefit, he explained, is a one-off payment of up to RM8,000 for recipients diagnosed with any of the 50 listed critical illnesses.
Fahmi also said beneficiaries are entitled to a medical device benefit of up to RM30,000.
According to figures presented to the Cabinet, he said over 1.294 million recipients have received hospitalisation benefits amounting to RM394.87 million.
“For critical illness claims, 89,085 recipients received payments amounting to RM712.68 million.
For medical device assistance, 13,128 recipients received support worth RM65.257 million,” he added.

Title: Amirudin says Selangor will act against all illegal settlements without considering nationality
Article:
KUALA SELANGOR, June 12 — The Selangor government will take action against all illegal settlements in the state, including demolition, regardless of the occupants’ nationality, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said.
He said enforcement measures are not based on sentiment or nationality but on land administration requirements, land-use conditions and compliance with local authority regulations.
“This issue is not about whether it involves Rohingya, Indonesians, Vietnamese, Nepalese or any other group.
“It is about administration and management, land approvals, land use and local authority approvals,” he told reporters after launching the Waste-to-Energy (WTE) 1 Plant Operations in Jeram here today.
Amirudin said the state government had issued notices and warnings to those involved before taking any enforcement action, adding that most cases involved breaches of land-use conditions or developments exceeding approved limits.
However, he said the state government must adhere to legal procedures, including giving landowners sufficient time to rectify breaches before stricter measures such as land forfeiture are imposed.
“The land forfeiture process is not a simple matter, so due legal procedures must be followed. However, I have received assurance from the District Officer that prompt action will be taken to demolish the illegal structures.
“The state government will continue to support and assist enforcement operations, including providing logistics such as trucks through local authorities to remove belongings or relocate the foreigners involved,” he said.
Amirudin said issues involving undocumented migrants fall under the jurisdiction of the Immigration Department and the Home Ministry, while the state government and local authorities stand ready to assist in terms of logistics as well as enforcement related to municipal regulations and land use.
Last Wednesday, Amirudin said various government agencies had been taking action since 2016 to address issues involving buildings occupied by the Rohingya community in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Hulu Langat, which recently went viral on social media.
On the same day, the Hulu Langat District and Land Office said demolition of the multi-storey residential building would be carried out in accordance with existing laws. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi: Putrajaya to collect detailed workforce data before deciding on foreign worker quotas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the government will gather more detailed workforce data before making any decisions on foreign worker quotas in the restaurant and construction sectors.
He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had directed several ministries to engage with industry players and obtain a clearer picture of labour requirements.
“As Malaysia currently maintains a policy on limiting the overall number of foreign workers, the prime minister directed the Human Resources Ministry, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, the Housing and Local Government Ministry, and the Works Ministry to obtain more detailed information from industry players regarding labour requirements, including demand for foreign workers,” he said.
He added that the information collected would be studied further before being brought back to the Cabinet for deliberation.
Last month, Anwar said Malaysia must reduce its reliance on foreign labour as it advances towards a higher value, more developed economy.
